what_it_does: The human/lead-written report (the REDUCE) over the g13_cluster.py output. Records the 2026-06-10 mining pass over 1267 exchange extracts (884 distinct tags clustered at 0.78 into 717 clusters), reports signal density, lists the top weighted named patterns and whether each maps to a standing memory rule, surfaces two BORDERLINE-NEW patterns as drafted-but-not-minted rules, and explains the queryable loop the report closes.
observations: headline is deliberately honest/anti-fabrication: the mining VALIDATES standing rules and did not find a new failure class
states this is the fourth independent mining pass (13 → 39 → 173 → 1267 extracts) with stable structure
signal density: 189/1267 exchanges carry a Tim correction (co-occurring with a named error), 30 frustration, 26 needs-tim, 21 bug-fix — ~1 in 7 is a teaching moment
top cluster is verify-by-use (weight 143, 16 variants merged), then verify-before-claiming (50)
table cross-references each pattern to an existing standing memory with checkmarks/warnings
two borderline-new patterns proposed with draft rule wording: describe-vs-request discrimination and build-and-show-don't-spec
quotes real corrections from the record verbatim
tail note: 700+ single-tag clusters are mostly flow vocabulary; conservative threshold under-merges by design
standouts: the anti-fabrication stance — reporting 'no new failure class' as the correct mirror result rather than inventing novelty
propose-don't-mint discipline for new rules: borderline patterns are drafted for Tim's gate, not auto-adopted
the verify-by-use / verify-before-claiming family is empirically the dominant pattern of the whole collaboration
conventions: follows the propose→gate shape described for everything else in the system
frames itself as a REDUCE over a live queryable history space
notes: The judgment-layer report companion to g13_cluster.py: a self-study confirming the collaboration's standing discipline rules empirically and drafting two new candidate rules for approval.
questions: Where are the 'standing feedback memories' this report cross-references actually stored?
What is the GC7 'diverse-panel pass' machinery referenced as not-yet-built?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it is the report that g13_cluster.py's docstring said the lead would write from clusters.json, and it does exactly that with cross-referencing and rule drafting.
apparent_themes: verify-by-use as the central collaboration discipline
intention_signals: Trying to close the self-study loop: turn the clustered mined history into an honest account of which discipline rules the record actually shows being taught, and to surface only genuinely-new patterns as gated drafts.
novelty: A system reporting on its OWN failure/correction patterns across its full interaction history, explicitly choosing confirmation over invented novelty, and treating new-rule adoption as a gated proposal.
contribution: Contributes the named weighted pattern taxonomy of the Tim-agent collaboration, the signal-density numbers, the mapping of patterns to standing memories, and two drafted candidate rules awaiting approval.
summary_for_embedding: A canonical update-in-place report reducing the G13 clustered mining of 1267 interaction extracts into weighted named collaboration patterns mapped to standing rules, plus two drafted new-rule candidates.
intention_for_embedding: To honestly self-study how the system works with Tim by confirming standing discipline rules empirically and proposing only genuinely-new patterns for his approval.
